verdict_reason: The single disagreement (rudhira-pradigdhān bhogān — 'tainted with blood enjoyments') is well-explained. It identifies that all translators render the phrase identically, but specifies the commentarial disagreement: whether the blood-taint is (a) metaphysical impurity attaching to the enjoyments themselves, OR (b) Arjuna's projected psychological inability to enjoy them. The doctrinal axis is named — this is precisely whether the verse describes an ontological or epistemic claim about the post-victory state. One tight line, doctrinally substantive, no winner picked. Gold-standard quality.
